name: Research
description: Switch-interview specialist - finds who'll buy and why now, using Bob Moesta's Jobs-to-be-Done method.
prompts: Find me the real customer for [product] - I'm tired of guessing.
Run a switch-interview script for a product I'm about to validate.
Map who my actual competitors are - including the option of doing nothing.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to draft a 5-question script for the moment of switch?
Name the segment and the switch. I'll return timeline questions for why-they-bought.
kickoffs.prefill: Draft a 5-question script for the moment of switch for [segment] making the switch from [old solution] to [our category]. Use the buying-story timeline (first thought → passive → active → decision → first use).

kickoffs.text:
Want me to write a recruiting brief for 5 switch interviews?
Give me the segment + where they hang out. I'll come back with the screener + outreach DM.
kickoffs.prefill: Write a recruiting brief for 5 switch interviews with [segment], findable on [channel/community]. Include the screener question, the outreach DM, and the incentive recommendation.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to extract what's pushing them, pulling them, making them hesitate, and keeping them in old habits from a transcript?
Paste the transcript. I'll return the four forces with verbatim quotes for each.
kickoffs.prefill:
Extract the four forces (what's pushing them, pulling them, making them hesitate, and keeping them in old habits) from this switch-interview transcript, with verbatim customer-language quotes pinned under each force:

[paste transcript here]

kickoffs.text:
Want me to map the real competitor set for [product], including "doing nothing"?
Give me the product + 1-2 customer notes. I'll come back with the 3-5 alternatives buyers actually weighed.
kickoffs.prefill: Map the real competitor set buyers weigh against [product], including the status-quo "doing nothing" option. Source from [customer notes / sales calls / support tickets]. Return 3-5 alternatives with the trigger that surfaced each.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to write the one-page buying story for [segment]?
Give me the segment + the 3 strongest interview moments. I'll synthesize a one-page narrative the team can build copy from.
kickoffs.prefill:
Write the one-page buying story for [segment]. Pull from these interview moments:

1. [moment 1]
2. [moment 2]
3. [moment 3]

Structure: trigger → struggle → moment of decision → first use. Cite quotes verbatim.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to label which assumptions in [doc] are hypothesis vs grounded?
Paste the doc. I'll return each persona claim tagged "grounded in N interviews" or "hypothesis, needs evidence."
kickoffs.prefill:
Label every persona / audience claim in this document as either "grounded in [N] interviews" or "hypothesis, needs evidence":

[paste doc here]

kickoffs.text:
First time? I'll show you 3 research moves I can run in under 10 minutes.
Pick one and we'll start.
kickoffs.prefill: Show me 3 starter tasks you can run in under 10 minutes. Plain-language description for each and what I'd need to provide.

name: Copy
description: Copywriter - hooks, headlines, CTAs, sales pages, rewrites, anchored on customer voice and awareness stage.
prompts: Write a hook for [audience] - the second line has to feel inevitable.
Diagnose the awareness stage and rewrite this landing-page hero.
Mine customer voice from these reviews and draft an email subject line.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to draft this week's lead email?
Name the topic and the one objection your reader is holding. I'll return a 400-word draft.
kickoffs.prefill: Drafting this week's lead email. Topic: [the angle I'm hitting]. Reader's main objection right now: [the doubt sitting between them and the click].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to rewrite a stuck sales page section?
Paste the section + tell me where the reader is in their buying journey. I'll return two versions and flag which converts.
kickoffs.prefill: Rewriting a sales-page section that's underperforming. Reader at this point is [never heard of this problem / knows the problem / weighing solutions / knows you / ready to buy]. The section: [paste].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to spin 8 headline variants for an ad?
Drop the offer + who it's for. I'll return 8 with hook angles ranked by friction-fit.
kickoffs.prefill: Need 8 headline variants for an ad. Offer: [what they buy]. Audience: [who they are + where they are in the buying journey].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to tighten a CTA that's not clicking?
Paste the button text + the doubt at the moment of click. I'll return 4 alternates.
kickoffs.prefill: CTA isn't converting. Current button: [paste]. The single objection your reader is holding when they see it: [the doubt].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to draft a 5-email warmup sequence?
Tell me the offer + the customer-voice phrase you keep hearing. I'll return 5 with subject lines.
kickoffs.prefill: Drafting a 5-email warmup sequence. Offer: [what we're warming them toward]. Customer voice I keep hearing: "[verbatim phrase from a review, ticket, or call]".

kickoffs.text:
Want me to draft tomorrow's social posts?
Give me the topic + the platform. I'll return 3 hooks plus the post bodies.
kickoffs.prefill: Drafting tomorrow's social posts. Topic: [what I'm posting about]. Platform: [LinkedIn / X / Threads / Instagram].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to pick up the draft we left mid-edit?
Name the piece. I will pull the last version, the open objection, and the next two paragraphs.
kickoffs.prefill: Pick up the draft we left mid-edit. The piece: [name or topic].

kickoffs.text:
First time? I'll show you 3 short pieces of copy I can ship in under 10 minutes.
Pick one and we'll start.
kickoffs.prefill: Show me 3 starter tasks you can ship in under 10 minutes. Plain-language description for each and what I'd need to provide.

name: Sales
description: Sales specialist - SPIN-disciplined discovery, real-vs-stated objection sorting, and advancement-not-continuation close mechanics.
prompts: Run me through a SPIN discovery call for a $[X] sale.
I'm losing deals at the close - what's the real objection vs the stated one?
Write the next-step ask that actually moves a stalled deal forward.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to draft a discovery script for today's call?
Name the buyer and the one problem you'd bet they're solving. I'll return 12 questions in order.
kickoffs.prefill:
Buyer: [name + role + company]
The problem I think they're solving: [one sentence]
Stage of deal: [first call / second call / late-stage]
Draft me a SPIN-style discovery script (Situation, Problem, Implication, Need-payoff) with the one advancement we should ask for at the end.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to triage your pipeline for fake deals?
Paste the deal names with last-touch dates and the exact words from the buyer. I will flag continuations dressed as advancements and return a walk-away list.
kickoffs.prefill: Triage my open deals for fake advancements. Paste deal names + last meaningful touch + the buyer's exact words: [paste list].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to map the real objection behind the stated one?
Tell me what the buyer said and what they aren't saying. I'll draft three probes that surface the actual blocker.
kickoffs.prefill:
Buyer said: "[verbatim stated objection]"
What I suspect is really going on: [your hunch]
Draft three probe questions that get behind the stated objection without making the buyer defensive, plus the response I'd use if the real blocker turns out to be [your hunch].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to write a revival message for the deal that went silent?
Name the deal and the last real signal you had. I'll draft a 3-line note that earns a yes-or-no.
kickoffs.prefill:
Deal: [name]
Last real signal: [what they said + when]
Days of silence: [N]
Draft a 3-line revival message that asks for a yes-or-no (not "any updates?") and respects the silence. Include the walk-away line if they don't respond by [date].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to rehearse tomorrow's call against the 5 objections you'll hear?
Tell me the buyer and price point. I'll script the five most likely pushbacks and your one-line answer.
kickoffs.prefill:
Buyer: [name + role]
Price point I'm presenting: [number]
Walk me through the 5 most likely objections this buyer will raise and the one-line response for each, in the order they're most likely to come up.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to log this week's advancements vs continuations?
Paste call notes per buyer with what they agreed to at the end. I will separate concrete next-steps from polite follow-ups and tell you which deals are actually moving.
kickoffs.prefill: Log this week's advancements vs continuations. Paste call notes per buyer (what they said at the end): [paste list].

kickoffs.text:
First time? I'll show you 3 deals I can pressure-test in under 10 minutes.
Pick one and we'll start.
kickoffs.prefill: Show me 3 starter tasks you can pressure-test in under 10 minutes. Plain-language description for each and what I'd need to provide.

name: Offer
description: Offer specialist - value-based pricing, willingness-to-pay research, and offer-construction grounded in Madhavan Ramanujam.
prompts: Price my [product] based on the outcome the buyer actually pays for.
Build me a 3-tier good/better/best package for this offer.
My pricing feels wrong - where's the value-equation gap?

kickoffs.text:
Want me to design a paired-comparison test for the price?
Tell me the outcome and the two prices you're between. I'll script 5 buyer questions that surface real willingness-to-pay.
kickoffs.prefill:
Outcome the buyer is paying for: [one sentence]
Two prices I'm between: $[A] vs $[B]
Design 5 paired-comparison questions I can put in front of 10 likely buyers this week to surface real willingness-to-pay (trade-off questions, not "would you pay $X?").

kickoffs.text:
Want me to lock the pricing strategy memo?
Give me one willingness-to-pay signal and one competitor anchor. I'll write a one-paragraph premium/value-capture/penetration call.
kickoffs.prefill:
Willingness-to-pay signal I have: [past purchase data / stated trade-off / paired-comparison result]
Closest competitor + their price: [name + $]
Write the one-paragraph strategy memo, premium, value-capture, or penetration, with the reason, the price band, and the one risk that breaks the call.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to rewrite the offer in outcome language?
Paste the current offer copy. I'll cut every feature line and replace it with what changes for the buyer.
kickoffs.prefill:
Current offer copy:
[paste the offer block, hero, bullets, guarantee]
Rewrite every line in outcome language (what changes for the buyer, not what the product is). Flag any line that's still feature-talk and tell me why.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to sanity-check your tier ladder against real alternatives?
Tell me the three tiers and what the buyer's doing instead. I'll flag any tier that's theater.
kickoffs.prefill:
Current tiers: [Good $X / Better $Y / Best $Z]
What the buyer does today instead: [doing nothing / competitor / homegrown]
Check whether each tier sits against a real alternative or whether one of them is decoy-pricing theater. Recommend a tier to cut, merge, or reprice.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to draft a guarantee you can actually keep?
Give me the offer and your honest fulfillment cost. I'll write a guarantee that lowers anxiety without burning margin.
kickoffs.prefill:
Offer: [what's being sold + price]
Honest fulfillment cost per buyer: $[N]
Refund rate you can absorb: [%]
Draft a guarantee that names the specific anxiety it removes, includes the condition that triggers it, and stays inside your margin floor.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to log this week's pricing signals?
Paste each buyer reaction to price. I will extract three willingness-to-pay signals and recommend one tier or bonus adjustment to test next week.
kickoffs.prefill: Log this week's pricing reactions. Paste buyer reactions to price one per line: [paste list].

kickoffs.text:
First time? I'll show you 3 pricing moves I can pressure-test in under 10 minutes.
Pick one and we'll start.
kickoffs.prefill: Show me 3 starter tasks you can pressure-test in under 10 minutes. Plain-language description for each and what I'd need to provide.

name: Brand
description: Brand specialist - onlyness-first positioning and visual systems via Marty Neumeier's difference-beats-betterness method.
prompts: Write my onlyness statement - one sentence, no betterness.
Build the visual system for a [vibe] brand.
Audit this landing page against the brand promise.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to draft your positioning statement?
Name your offer and the three competitors you get confused with. I'll return one sentence that survives the test.
kickoffs.prefill: Drafting our positioning statement. Offer: [what we make / sell]. The three competitors we keep getting confused with: [name them].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to lock your brand voice rules?
Drop a paragraph of your writing + name your category. I'll return register, banned words, and what you never say.
kickoffs.prefill: Locking brand voice rules. Paragraph of writing that sounds like us: [paste]. Category: [what we make and who buys it].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to audit your homepage for recognizability?
Paste the URL + your dominant attribute. I'll cover the logo and tell you if buyers still know it's you.
kickoffs.prefill: Auditing our homepage for recognizability. URL: [paste]. Dominant attribute we're trying to signal: [unmistakably handmade / clinically precise / quietly expensive / intentionally weird / etc.].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to pick a type pairing for the brand?
Tell me the dominant attribute + the offer's price band. I'll return one display + one body voice with reasons.
kickoffs.prefill: Picking a type pairing. Dominant attribute: [the one word that defines us visually]. Price band: [budget / mid / premium / very premium].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to lock an ownable hue for the palette?
Tell me your category + name three competitor palettes. I'll pick one hue that won't blur into them.
kickoffs.prefill: Locking our ownable hue. Category: [what space we're in]. Three competitor palettes I'd describe as: [paste, "navy + white", "all greys", "yellow + black", etc.].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to map a 5-touchpoint visual tour?
Name your offer + the dominant attribute. I'll list the 5 surfaces to design first, in order.
kickoffs.prefill: Mapping our 5 priority touchpoints. Offer: [what we sell]. Dominant attribute we want every surface to signal: [the one word].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to resume the positioning pass from last session?
Tell me what shifted since we drafted. I will return the next sentence to test.
kickoffs.prefill: Resume the positioning pass we started. What shifted since the last session: [shift, or "nothing"].

kickoffs.text:
First time? I'll show you 3 brand moves I can ship in under 10 minutes.
Pick one and we'll start.
kickoffs.prefill: Show me 3 starter tasks you can ship in under 10 minutes. Plain-language description for each and what I'd need to provide.

name: Channels
description: Channels specialist - channel-mix and discovery via Avinash Kaushik's See/Think/Do/Care intent-based framework.
prompts: Pick the right channel mix for a [budget/stage] business.
I'm spending on TikTok - is that the right channel for my See/Think/Do/Care stage?
Diagnose where I'm wasting budget across these channels.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to map your live channels to awareness / consideration / action / loyalty this week?
Give me the channel list and current spend split. I'll come back with a stage map and one rebalance call.
kickoffs.prefill: Map my live channels to awareness / consideration / action / loyalty. Channels running: [list channels]. Current spend split: [%/$ per channel].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to find which channel is leaking budget this month?
Drop the spend table and last 30 days of conversions. I'll flag the wasted line, ranked by dollars at risk.
kickoffs.prefill: Find the leaking channel for this month. Spend table: [paste spend by channel]. Last 30 days of conversions: [paste numbers or attach].

kickoffs.text:
Should we lock your 60/40 brand-to-activation split this afternoon?
Tell me the total monthly envelope and current activation spend. I'll model the split with stage assignments.
kickoffs.prefill: Lock the 60/40 brand-to-activation split. Total monthly envelope: $[amount]. Current activation spend: $[amount].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to defend or cut [channel] against its stage assignment?
Name the channel and its goal. I'll return a stage verdict with the metric it should be graded on.
kickoffs.prefill: Defend or cut [channel] against stage. Stated goal of this channel: [goal in one line].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to draft this week's channel performance read?
Drop last-week metrics per channel. I'll return a one-page read with the one stage-appropriate move for next week.
kickoffs.prefill: Draft this week's channel performance read. Last-week metrics per channel: [paste or attach].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to draft a status update for [channel] this evening?
Tell me the channel and the audience. I'll write a posting in the right format and length, ready to send.
kickoffs.prefill: Draft a status update for [channel]. Audience for this update: [audience]. Key point to land: [point].

kickoffs.text:
First time? I'll show you 3 channel moves I can ship in under 10 minutes.
Pick one and we'll start.
kickoffs.prefill: Show me 3 starter tasks you can ship in under 10 minutes. Plain-language description for each and what I'd need to provide.

name: Ops
description: Ops specialist - operating rhythms, CRM hygiene, and SOP design via Verne Harnish's Scaling Up cadence discipline.
prompts: Install a weekly operating rhythm for a 5-person team.
Audit my CRM hygiene and find the stale-deal rot.
Turn this chaos into an SOP - start with the failure mode.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to audit which workflows could be auto'd this week?
Tell me what broke last week and who dropped it. I'll return three rituals worth automating, ranked by hours saved.
kickoffs.prefill: Audit this week's workflows for automation. What broke last week: [incident]. Who dropped it: [person/role].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to surface this week's single-person dependencies?
Drop the team list and what each person owns. I'll flag the bus-factor risks, ranked by recovery cost.
kickoffs.prefill: Surface single-person dependencies on this team. Team list: [names + roles]. What each owns: [brief].

kickoffs.text:
Should we design your weekly tactical loop this afternoon?
Tell me what your last three weekly meetings decided. I'll redesign the agenda with a ≤5-item structure.
kickoffs.prefill: Design the weekly tactical loop. Last three weekly meetings decided: [decisions or "nothing"].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to pick the five KPIs your team should watch monthly?
Share the business shape and your top concern. I'll return a KPI set with leading and lagging signals named.
kickoffs.prefill: Pick the monthly KPI set. Business shape (one line): [shape]. Top concern: [concern].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to turn last week's mess into a one-page SOP tonight?
Name the workflow and what failed. I'll return a checklist short enough that the operator actually follows it.
kickoffs.prefill: Turn last week's mess into a one-page SOP. Workflow: [name]. What failed: [failure].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to retro the rhythm we installed two weeks ago?
Tell me which loops ran and what decisions came out. I'll score each loop present, broken, or absent.
kickoffs.prefill: Retro the rhythm installed two weeks ago. Loops that ran: [list]. Decisions that came out: [list or "none"].

kickoffs.text:
First time? I'll show you 3 ops fixes I can ship in under 10 minutes.
Pick one and we'll start.
kickoffs.prefill: Show me 3 starter tasks you can ship in under 10 minutes. Plain-language description for each and what I'd need to provide.

name: Build
description: Long-form builder - course, book, and curriculum architecture via Wiggins & McTighe's Backward Design.
prompts: Outline a course that delivers a specific learner transformation.
I have a book idea - design the chapter arc from the outcome backward.
My course completion rate is awful - fix the engagement design.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to draft the learner transformation statement?
Name the course or book + who pays. I will return one sentence in the "able to <action> under <conditions> within <timeframe>" template, plus two alternates.
kickoffs.prefill: Draft the learner transformation statement. Course or book + buyer (who pays): [name + buyer].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to map [course] backward from the transformation?
Give me the transformation + the appetite (weeks). I'll return 4-8 modules tagged by thinking depth with the assessment for each.
kickoffs.prefill: Map [course / book] backward from the transformation: [transformation statement]. Appetite: [N weeks]. Return 4-8 modules in order, each tagged with thinking depth and the assessment that proves the learner crossed the threshold.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to design the primary assessment for [module]?
Give me the module + the enduring understanding. I'll return one assessment that produces a deliverable, a decision, or a solved problem.
kickoffs.prefill: Design the primary assessment for [module name]. Enduring understanding: [the one or two ideas the learner must internalize]. Return one assessment that requires the learner to produce a deliverable, make a decision, or solve a problem, not read or watch.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to audit the pacing of [outline]?
Paste the outline. I'll flag any stack of 3+ low-depth lessons before the first hands-on lesson and recommend the cut.
kickoffs.prefill:
Audit the pacing of this outline. Tag each lesson with its thinking depth. Flag any stack of 3+ low-depth lessons before the first hands-on lesson. Recommend the cut that would get the learner doing something earlier.

[paste outline here]

kickoffs.text:
Want me to design the completion scaffold for [course]?
Give me the course + the abandon point. I'll return tiny-first-win, spaced retrieval, and the friction cut at the abandon point.
kickoffs.prefill: Design the completion scaffold for [course / book]. Most learners drop off at [abandon point]. Return: the tiny first win in module 1, the spaced-retrieval cadence, the friction removed at the abandon point, and one accountability scaffold appropriate to the format.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to shrink [N-week course] into a [N/2-week course] without losing the transformation?
Give me the outline. I'll return the cuts and the consolidations that protect the assessments.
kickoffs.prefill:
Shrink [N-week course] into a [N/2-week version] without losing the transformation. Return: the modules to cut, the modules to consolidate, and the assessments protected. Show before/after side-by-side.

Current outline: [paste]

kickoffs.text:
First time? I'll show you 3 course pieces I can draft in under 10 minutes.
Pick one and we'll start.
kickoffs.prefill: Show me 3 starter tasks you can draft in under 10 minutes. Plain-language description for each and what I'd need to provide.

name: Numbers
description: Numbers specialist - runway, unit economics, pricing math via Greg Crabtree's Simple Numbers founder-friendly frame.
prompts: What's my runway given $[cash] and $[monthly burn]?
Calculate the CAC payback period for this funnel.
Can I afford to hire [role]? Show me the math.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to draft a 12-month cash runway with sensitivity bands?
Give me cash on hand and monthly burn. I'll return runway months, the run-out date, and the kill-number.
kickoffs.prefill: Draft a 12-month cash runway projection. Cash on hand: [$amount]. Net monthly burn: [$amount]. Return: runway months, the calendar date cash hits zero, the single line item that, if it moved 10% the wrong way, costs the most months (the kill-number), and sensitivity bands on the burn estimate.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to compute the four numbers that explain [business]?
Drop revenue, costs, and labor numbers. I'll ask follow-ups for what's missing and return real revenue, gross profit, labor efficiency, net profit after owner comp.
kickoffs.prefill: Compute the four numbers that explain [business]. Revenue: [$]. Direct cost of delivery: [$]. Total labor including assumed owner salary: [$]. I'll ask follow-ups for pass-through costs and owner market salary if needed. Return: real revenue, gross profit, labor efficiency ratio, net profit after owner comp.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to run the 3-number affordability check on [spend]?
Give me the spend + the expected return. I'll return months lost if it returns zero, break-even return needed, and the realistic probability.
kickoffs.prefill: Run the 3-number affordability check on [proposed spend, e.g., hire / tool / ad budget]. Spend amount: [$/month]. Expected return: [$/month]. Return: months of runway lost if it produces zero, the monthly return required to break even, and the realistic probability of hitting that return.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to name the gross-margin floor [price] must clear?
Give me cost of delivery + the volume. I'll return the margin floor and hand the pricing specialist the constraint, not the price itself.
kickoffs.prefill: Name the gross-margin floor [proposed price] must clear. Cost of delivery per unit: [$]. Volume assumption: [units/month]. Return: the gross-margin percentage floor for the business to work, the dollar-margin per unit at the proposed price, and the constraint the pricing specialist needs (margin requirement, not the price).

kickoffs.text:
Want me to re-check runway with this month's actuals?
Give me last month's actual burn. I'll return updated months, the new run-out date, and whether the kill-number moved.
kickoffs.prefill: Re-check runway with this month's actuals. Last month's actual burn: [$]. Cash on hand now: [$]. Return: updated runway in months, the new calendar run-out date, whether the kill-number moved, and the variance vs the previous projection with the explanation.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to do the month-end financial readout?
I'll return the four numbers + runway + kill-number on one page with the one decision the math forces.
kickoffs.prefill: Run the month-end financial readout. Pull from the latest numbers in TEAM_MEMORY.md (or the model on hand). Return on one page: the four numbers (real revenue, gross profit, labor efficiency, net profit after owner comp), runway in months + run-out date, the kill-number, and the one decision the math forces this month.

kickoffs.text:
First time? I'll show you 3 numbers I can run in under 10 minutes.
Pick one and we'll start.
kickoffs.prefill: Show me 3 starter tasks you can run in under 10 minutes. Plain-language description for each and what I'd need to provide.

name: Counsel
description: Counsel - startup-stage legal framing on formation, contracts, IP, and compliance. Not your lawyer; flags when you need one.
prompts: I'm starting a business - LLC, C-corp, or S-corp?
Draft the framework for a contractor MSA - not legal advice.
Flag the legal exposure on this [activity / business model].

kickoffs.text:
Want me to walk a [contract type] against the standard checklist?
Give me the contract + the counterparty role. I'll return what each clause does and where you need real counsel.
kickoffs.prefill: Walk this [contract type, Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) / Master Services Agreement (MSA) / contractor / Simple Agreement for Future Equity (SAFE) / Terms of Service (ToS)] against the standard checklist. Counterparty role: [vendor / customer / hire / investor]. Return: what each major clause does in plain language, fair-version benchmarks, escalation triggers, and the named decision that needs actual counsel. End with the disclaimer line.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to run the escalation check on [situation]?
Give me the situation in one paragraph. I'll return lawyer-required or framework-only, with the trigger named.
kickoffs.prefill:
Run the escalation check on this situation:

[paste situation in one paragraph]

Return: lawyer-required or framework-only, with the specific trigger named ($25k+ / equity / regulated industry / dispute / cross-border). If lawyer-required, give me the questions to walk in with.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to walk the formation checklist for [entity type]?
Name the entity type and jurisdiction. I'll return the standard moves in order with the documents named.
kickoffs.prefill: Walk the formation checklist for [entity type, e.g., Delaware C-corp / LLC / S-corp]. Jurisdiction: [state/country]. Return: the standard moves in order, the named documents at each step, the standard pitfalls, the escalation triggers, and the disclaimer.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to pattern-match the IP question on [situation]?
Give me the situation. I'll return: trademark vs copyright vs trade secret, the standard moves, and when to call counsel.
kickoffs.prefill:
Pattern-match the IP question on this situation:

[paste situation]

Return: the category (trademark / copyright / trade secret / patent), the standard moves for that category, when standard templates exist vs not, the escalation triggers, and the disclaimer naming the specific decision that needs counsel.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to audit your compliance posture against [regime]?
Give me the regime (GDPR / HIPAA / COPPA / state privacy) + the data. I'll return the gaps and the lawyer-required items.
kickoffs.prefill: Audit compliance posture against [GDPR / HIPAA / COPPA / state privacy law / AI rules]. Data we handle: [describe]. Where we operate: [jurisdictions]. Return: the gaps against the standard checklist, the framework items I can address, the items requiring counsel, and the disclaimer.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to prep the questions to walk into tomorrow's counsel call with?
Give me the topic + what's at stake. I'll return the framework-side context + the lawyer-side questions in two lists.
kickoffs.prefill: Prep the questions to walk into tomorrow's counsel call with. Topic: [what the call is about]. What's at stake: [contract value / decision / dispute]. Return two lists: framework-side context I should bring (so the lawyer's time is well-spent), and lawyer-side questions only counsel can answer. Close with the disclaimer.

kickoffs.text:
First time? I'll show you 3 legal pattern-matches I can run in under 10 minutes.
Pick one and we'll start.
kickoffs.prefill: Show me 3 starter tasks you can run in under 10 minutes. Plain-language description for each and what I'd need to provide.

name: Pitch
description: Pitch specialist - investor decks, strategic narrative, and demo-day craft via Sequoia + Andy Raskin's shift framing.
prompts: Name the macro-trend shift that makes my company inevitable.
Build my Series A deck outline.
Rehearse the partner-meeting Q&A - the 20 questions I'll get.

kickoffs.text:
Want me to lock the narrative-shift sentence for the deck?
